UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the LearnStream application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last login.

activeSubscriptionId.description: Reference to the user's currently active Subscription. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 Subscription)

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents a learning course offered on the LearnStream platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the course content and objectives.

imageUrl.description: URL to the course's thumbnail or banner image.

isFree.description: Indicates if the course is freely accessible without a subscription.

difficultyLevel.description: The perceived difficulty level of the course (e.g., 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Advanced').

instructorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the instructor who created or teaches this course. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Course)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the course was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the course was last updated.

Module.title: Module
Module.description: Represents a logical grouping of lessons within a specific course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Module entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this module belongs to. (Relationship: Course 1:N Module)

description.description: A brief description of the module's content.

order.description: The sequential order of the module within its course.

Lesson.title: Lesson
Lesson.description: Represents a single unit of learning content within a module.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lesson entity.

moduleId.description: Reference to the Module this lesson belongs to. (Relationship: Module 1:N Lesson)

description.description: A brief description or summary of the lesson's content.

order.description: The sequential order of the lesson within its module.

durationInMinutes.description: The estimated duration of the lesson in minutes.

isPremium.description: Indicates if this specific lesson requires a premium subscription, potentially overriding the course's 'isFree' status.

Resource.title: Resource
Resource.description: Represents a specific piece of content (e.g., video, text, external link) associated with a lesson.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Resource entity.

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson this resource belongs to. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N Resource)

contentUrl.description: URL for video content, external links, or downloadable files.

contentText.description: Textual content, typically for 'text' type resources.

order.description: The sequential order of the resource within its lesson.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the resource was created.

SubscriptionPlan.title: SubscriptionPlan
SubscriptionPlan.description: Defines different subscription tiers available to users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SubscriptionPlan entity.

name.description: The name of the subscription plan (e.g., 'Monthly Premium', 'Annual Pro').

description.description: A description of the plan's benefits and features.

price.description: The price of the subscription plan.

currency.description: The currency of the subscription price (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

durationInDays.description: The duration of the subscription plan in days (e.g., 30, 365).

features.description: A list of features included in this subscription plan.

Subscription.title: Subscription
Subscription.description: Tracks a user's active or historical subscription to a plan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subscription entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this subscription. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Subscription)

planId.description: Reference to the SubscriptionPlan for this subscription. (Relationship: SubscriptionPlan 1:1 Subscription)

status.description: The current status of the subscription (e.g., 'active', 'cancelled', 'expired', 'pending').

startDate.description: The date when the subscription started.

endDate.description: The date when the subscription is set to end or has ended.

autoRenew.description: Indicates if the subscription is set to auto-renew.

paymentProviderSubscriptionId.description: The identifier for this subscription within the integrated payment gateway (e.g., Stripe).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription record was last updated.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records details of a financial transaction, typically related to subscription payments.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made the transaction. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Transaction)

subscriptionId.description: Reference to the Subscription this transaction is for, if applicable. (Relationship: Subscription 1:N Transaction)

amount.description: The amount of money involved in the transaction.

currency.description: The currency of the transaction amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

transactionDate.description: The date and time when the transaction occurred.

paymentMethodType.description: The type of payment method used (e.g., 'Credit Card', 'PayPal', 'Bank Transfer').

paymentProviderTransactionId.description: The identifier for this transaction within the integrated payment gateway (e.g., Stripe).

CourseProgress.title: CourseProgress
CourseProgress.description: Tracks a specific user's progress within a particular course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CourseProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile tracking their progress. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Course via CourseProgress)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course being tracked. (Relationship: Course N:N UserProfile via CourseProgress)

progressPercentage.description: The overall percentage of the course completed by the user (0-100).

lastAccessedLessonId.description: Reference to the last Lesson the user accessed in this course. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N CourseProgress)

completedLessonIds.description: An array of IDs of lessons that the user has completed in this course.

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the user started the course.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the user completed the course, if applicable.

status.description: The current status of the user's progress in the course (e.g., 'not_started', 'in_progress', 'completed').

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID `{userId}` matches `request.auth.uid`. Includes 'isPremiumSubscriber' boolean, updated by backend, for subscription access checks.
Subcollection for a user's subscription records. `userId` matches parent `{userId}`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (owner of the subscription).

params.name: subscriptionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the subscription.

definition.description: Subcollection for a user's financial transactions. `userId` matches parent `{userId}`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (initiator of the transaction).

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the transaction.

definition.description: Subcollection tracking a specific user's progress in a given course. The document ID `{courseId}` maps directly to the Course being tracked, simplifying unique access per user-course pair. `userId` matches parent `{userId}`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user tracking progress.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the course, also used as the document ID for CourseProgress.

definition.description: Stores definitions of various subscription plans. Publicly readable.

params.name: planId
params.description: The unique identifier of the subscription plan.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all courses. Contains `instructorId` for ownership checks. All documents are publicly readable (metadata).

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the course.

definition.description: Modules within a specific course. Includes denormalized `instructorId` (from Course) and `courseIsFree` (from Course's isFree property) for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent course.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the module.

definition.description: Lessons within a specific module. Includes denormalized `instructorId` (from Course), `courseIsFree` (from Course's isFree property), and its own `isPremium` for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent course.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent module.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lesson.

definition.description: Resources associated with a specific lesson. Includes denormalized `instructorId` (from Course), `courseIsFree` (from Course's isFree property), and `lessonIsPremium` (from Lesson's isPremium property) for authorization independence.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent course.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent module.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent lesson.

params.name: resourceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the resource.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for LearnStream prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. The design leverages path-based ownership for user-specific data and a hierarchical subcollection structure for course content, enhanced with crucial denormalization.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) via Denormalization:**
To eliminate `get()` calls in security rules, especially for authorization decisions involving parent documents, the following denormalization strategy is applied:
1.  **Instructor Write Access:** For course content (Modules, Lessons, Resources), the `instructorId` from the parent `Course` document will be denormalized and stored directly within each `Module`, `Lesson`, and `Resource` document. This allows security rules for these subcollections to directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.instructorId` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `Course` document. This is critical for robust atomic operations and simplified rule debugging.
2.  **Course/Lesson Read Access (Free/Premium Status):**
    *   The `isFree` status from the `Course` document will be denormalized as `courseIsFree` into all child `Module`, `Lesson`, and `Resource` documents.
    *   The `isPremium` status from the `Lesson` document will be denormalized as `lessonIsPremium` into its child `Resource` documents.
    *   For the user's subscription status, a dedicated boolean field, `isPremiumSubscriber`, will be added to the `UserProfile` document. This field will be updated by a Cloud Function whenever a user's `Subscription` status changes. This allows read rules for course content to check `get(/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.isPremiumSubscriber` which is a `get()` call to the *current user's own profile*, a commonly accepted and efficient pattern, rather than a `get()` to a related (non-parent) collection or complex, chained `get()` operations. This ensures that authorization logic does not rely on arbitrary parent document data or complex lookups during rule evaluation.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) Support:**
1.  **Public Collections:** Collections like `/courses` (for course metadata) and `/subscriptionPlans` allow `list` operations for all authenticated users, with rules ensuring data integrity and potentially basic authentication (`request.auth.uid != null`).
2.  **Private User Data:** Collections under `/users/{userId}/...` (e.g., `subscriptions`, `transactions`, `courseProgress`) inherently support QAPs because the `list` operation is implicitly filtered by `request.auth.uid`. Rules like `allow list: request.auth.uid == userId` ensure users can only list their own data.
3.  **Course Content (Modules, Lessons, Resources):** The denormalized `courseIsFree` and `lessonIsPremium` fields directly on the `Module`, `Lesson`, and `Resource` documents, combined with the `isPremiumSubscriber` field on the user's `UserProfile`, allow security rules to evaluate read access directly on each document. This structure prevents unauthorized documents from being returned in `list` operations. For instance, a rule for lessons could be `allow read: request.auth.uid != null && (resource.courseIsFree || (resource.isPremium && get(/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.isPremiumSubscriber));`. This ensures that only free lessons or premium lessons for subscribers are returned, satisfying QAP requirements without client-side filtering or inefficient rule logic.
